title: About the Little Girl that Beat Her Sister by Ann Taylor — Love Poetry Lines
content:
Go, go, my naughty girl, and kiss
Your little sister dear;
I must not have such things as this,
And noisy quarrels here.

What! little children scratch and fight,
That ought to be so mild;
Oh! Mary, it's a shocking sight
meaning: "About the Little Girl that Beat Her Sister" by Ann Taylor is a love poem consisting of 24 lines. The poet explores themes of love through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Go, go, my naughty girl, and kiss Your little sister dear;...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Ann Taylor's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
